column1: # Per-file canonical extra HS flags, beyond bare `--prove`.
# WHY: some theories are meant to be run with specific flags (Makefile
# case-study recipes / in-file invocation comments). Running them bare makes
# HS time out or produce NO output (SKIP), so we never recorded the real HS
# behaviour and "lost track" of what they need. This map records the canonical
# flags so the HS cache is generated the right way, and the SAME flags are
# passed to RS for the comparison.
# Consumed by corpus_file_diff.sh and diff_proof_raw.sh. The cache key is
# salted with a hash of the flags, so flagged entries are distinct from the
# bare cache (flagless files are completely unaffected — no cache churn).
# `fromMaybe CutDFS`), so bare `--prove` already == dfs — do NOT list it here.
#   --diff               RS NOT PORTED (errors "not yet ported"); HS recorded,
#                        RS can't match → kept out of parity_corpus.txt.
#   --auto-sources       routed (RS generates the sources lemma); the spore /
#                        AUTO_SOURCES_CASE_STUDIES rows below are corpus MATCHes.
#   --stop-on-trace=...  dfs (default) and seqdfs are routed in prove-mode;
#   in-file `heuristic: o "..."` oracles need NO entry here — RS routes the
#                        in-file heuristic + has oracle_ranking, so those files
# ---- auto-sources spore models (HS empty bare; need --auto-sources) ----------
# ---- seqdfs-dependent regression (HS empty/wrong under default dfs) ----------
# ---- Makefile:157). Added 2026-07-09 after the skip-class census; the
# ---- remaining 44 gate skips are upstream-excluded and were removed
# ---- cwd-dependent oracle (upstream deforacle recipe cd-s into the dir,
# ---- Makefile:199-201; the default-oracle lookup is cwd-relative) --------
# ---- note, lines 7-8). seqdfs is semantically load-bearing: step counts
# ---- differ from the default dfs. The in-file
# ---- `heuristic: o "./oracle-dmn-message-tracing"` needs NO flag and no
# ---- @cd: HS resolves the ./ path relative to the theory file's directory.
# first-3/4/5 exceed FILE_TIMEOUT=300 in HS under these canonical flags and
# are excluded from the corpus; rows kept commented so the canonical
